title: Visitacion Valley Reviews - Niche
content: * Neighborhood in San Francisco, CA. I have not had too many issues growing up here and have been able to become friends with my neighbors. It's a relatively convenient and cheap place to live in, with many lines of buses that go downtown and easy access to freeways, but it's not the safest place to live. I live in this neighborhood and find it to be family oriented, peaceful and safe. The weather is very sunny compared to the rest of San Francisco and you get stunning views of the Bay and San Bruno hills. Located on the southeast side of San Francisco, Visitacion Valley is a smaller community that's known for being close to the Cow Palace and the former 49ers stadium, Candlestick Park. Visitacion Valley is filled with sunny days, cheap food, and nice people and families who have been living here for decades. I like to live in San Francisco because the weather in here is suitable for people to live in.

title: Visitacion Knob - Bob Burd's Trip Reports
content: None of them required trespassing. The weather today was quite fine for riding, hiking or walking, some overcast but neither hot nor cold. Views

title: Visitacion Knob - hikes and trails to get you there - AllTrails
content: A great trail in the heart of the city! Just discovered that it's San Francisco's second largest park today. Paved and dirt paths with lots of shaded areas.

title: San Francisco's Under-The-Radar 'Secret Neighborhood' Has Urban Trails, Cafes, And Affordable Eats
content: San Francisco's Under-The-Radar 'Secret Neighborhood' Has Urban Trails, Cafes, And Affordable Eats. If you're like me, San Francisco's Visitacion Valley might not have crossed your mind as a place to visit. As a Bay Area resident who spends a lot of time in The City with friends and family, I can count on one hand the number of times I've visited this neighborhood near Daly City's underrated shops and parks. Although still residential, a collection of cozy cafes and Mexican, Colombian, Chinese, and Vietnamese restaurants (with shockingly affordable prices for San Francisco) line Leland Avenue. A mural of tropical plants painted on the facade of a Colombian Mexican restaurant in San Francisco. Visitacion Valley has gained a reputation as a "secret neighborhood" not because it's particularly hard to find, but because it hasn't made the rounds on travel blogs or social media, unlike San Francisco's famous Mission District. A path winds through an urban garden filled with California native plants in Visitacion Valley, San Francisco.
